task_completion.description: Open Vital Recorder and load the .vital case files
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing the Vital Recorder application open with at least one of the target files (0001.vital, 0002.vital, or 0003.vital) loaded into the interface.

task_completion.description: Locate and view the event markers for the loaded cases
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing the event list, timeline, or event markers (e.g., 'Case started', 'Surgery started', 'Surgery finished', 'Case finished') visible within the Vital Recorder application.

task_completion.description: Export the vital signs data to the required CSV files
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing the export dialog in Vital Recorder saving files as 'case_0001_events.csv', 'case_0002_events.csv', and 'case_0003_events.csv', or these three CSV files visible on the Desktop.

task_completion.description: Create the event timeline database text file
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing a text editor (like Notepad) open with a new file, or a file named 'event_timeline_db.txt' visible on the Desktop.

task_completion.description: Structure the database with the correct columns, events, and phases
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots of the open 'event_timeline_db.txt' file showing tabular data with columns for Case_ID, Event_Name, Time_from_Start_min, and Phase (pre-operative, intraoperative, post-operative), populated with the extracted events.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Vital Recorder GUI to extract data
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show active interaction with the Vital Recorder application windows, menus, and export dialogs, rather than bypassing the UI using terminal scripts to parse the binary files.

integrity.description: Verify the CSV files were genuinely exported from the application
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the application's export progress or save dialogs, ensuring the CSVs were not just created as empty dummy files via terminal commands (e.g., 'touch case_0001_events.csv').

integrity.description: Verify the database was manually constructed or genuinely populated
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent typing, pasting, or formatting the extracted data into the text editor, rather than using a single terminal command to echo a hardcoded string of the expected final answer.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires extracting exactly 12 events across the three cases (4 standard events per case: 'Case started', 'Surgery started', 'Surgery finished', and 'Case finished'). The expected outputs are three CSV files ('case_0001_events.csv', 'case_0002_events.csv', 'case_0003_events.csv') on the Desktop containing exported vital signs data, and one text file ('event_timeline_db.txt') on the Desktop. The text database must be tabular and contain four specific columns: Case_ID, Event_Name, Time_from_Start_min, and Phase (which must classify the event as pre-operative, intraoperative, or post-operative).
